user_prompt: Inside the `ScrollView` for Wi-Fi, the user can click on a `ScrollItem` representing a Wi-Fi router or initiate a scan-and-connect. This should lead to `ConnWaitingView`.
If the user cancels, the user should return to the `ScrollView` for Wi-Fi.
If the connection succeeds. There are two scenarios: at least one cloud upload destination is available, or none are available.

Create a `WifiStaModeView` much like `WifiApModeView`, borrow the same rough layout. There does not need to be a security icon. The SSID can always be shown in the same place and fonts and colour. The password is never shown. There does not need to be a show/hide button. The text should show “assigned IP:” and “gateway IP:”
The function where statistics are shown and cycled is kept.

If no cloud destinations are available, then `WifiStaModeView`  only shows the text about how you need to reset or power off to stop. Otherwise, there is a button `FlyViewItem` using `sprite_canceldoor_50` to dismiss the dialog to go back to the `ScrollView`, put this in the bottom right corner and tie it to `BtnC`.
If there is at least one cloud upload destination, then the `ScrollView` for cloud items will have an clickable item for just “information”, clicking that one leads to `WifiStaModeView`
Exiting out of the `ScrollView` for cloud items causes the microcontroller to reset.
agent_reply: Added [WifiStaModeView.h](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/WifiStaModeView.h>) and [WifiStaModeView.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGuiViews/WifiStaModeView.cpp>): it shows STA mode, SSID, assigned IP, gateway IP, and the same cycling web stats style as AP mode. When cloud destinations exist, it shows the `sprite_canceldoor_50` dismiss button on BtnC/bottom-right back to the cloud `ScrollView`; otherwise it shows the reset/power-off stop hint.
- Wi-Fi station click and scan-and-connect now go to `ConnWaitingView`.
- Cancel disconnects Wi-Fi and returns to the Wi-Fi `ScrollView`.
- Successful station connect starts the web server, then shows cloud `ScrollView` if destinations exist, otherwise `WifiStaModeView`.
- Cloud `ScrollView` has an `Information` item that opens `WifiStaModeView`.
- Exiting the cloud `ScrollView` resets the device.

Verification: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passes, and `git diff --check` is clean. I restored the build-generated `src/version.c` change; there is an unrelated existing change in `tools/check_dev_pace.py` that I left alone.

user_prompt: also make sure every `.cpp` file inside `lib\FlyGuiViews` meet the guidelines at `developer-docs\code-layout-policy.md`, however, it is also important that their code layout look almost the same between them as they do kind of inherit from the same base classes sometimes
agent_reply: The common shape is now: constants/types/prototypes first, primary view/API methods next, supporting/drawing/formatting helpers later. I also kept the FlyGui view files visually aligned with each other by using the same lightweight section markers and putting helper blocks at the bottom.
- `src\version.c` build churn was restored; final status only shows the intended layout files.

The common shape is now: constants/types/prototypes first, primary view/API methods next, supporting/drawing/formatting helpers later. I also kept the FlyGui view files visually aligned with each other by using the same lightweight section markers and putting helper blocks at the bottom.

user_prompt: edit `lib\WifiManager\WifiManager.cpp`, I don't like it being a class object, turn it into a single instance namespace module.
there's a lot of code that looks like `(!wifi_manager)` that is pointless, as if it could be missing, allocating it is not a good idea, it can be totally avoided.
so I want any code that currently looks like `wifi_manager->` to look more like `WifiManager::`
the behavior needs to stay the same, that shouldn't be hard, we never had more than one instance of this thing anyways
It would make the recovery-mode Wi-Fi launch much more easy to think about
agent_reply: Done. `WifiManager` is now a single-instance namespace module instead of an allocated class object.

- Converted [WifiManager.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.h) / [WifiManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WifiManager.cpp) from class state to namespace API + file-scope module state.
- Removed the global `wifi_manager` pointer, heap allocation, `extern` declarations, and pointless `if (!wifi_manager)` branches.
- Updated `ScrollView`, `NtpSync`, web handlers, Wi-Fi views, error recovery AP launch, and the affected tests.
- Preserved the old constructor side effect by calling `WifiManager::clear()` during boot and in standalone AP tests.
All passed. PlatformIO touched `src/version.c` during builds; I left it as-is.

user_prompt: I want to change all `void redraw(bool forced)` to `bool redraw(bool forced)`, and track if anything was actually redrawn from the top level `void FlyGui::poll()` with `bool drawn = redraw(false);`, this bool `drawn` will later be used for a statistic diagnosis
agent_reply: Done. I changed the FlyGui redraw contract to return `bool`, with [FlyGui::poll](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/FlyGui/FlyGui.cpp:158) now capturing:

The base `FlyGui`, `FlyGuiView`, and `FlyGuiItem` paths now aggregate whether display work happened, and the relevant overrides in FlyGui text/progress, views, PIN pad, and WAV playback now return `true` when they actually redraw and `false` when they skip.
- `rg` found no remaining `void redraw(bool forced)` under `lib`, `src`, or `inc`.

Only build warning was the existing third-party `ESP-FTP-Server` `NetworkServer::available()` deprecation. PlatformIO also touched `src/version.c` during the builds; I left existing unrelated dirty files alone.
